notes: v1.14.35 — entry space de-clutter & star de-cheese (brief: PHANTOM-space-declutter). Cut A removed the planetary/orbital ornaments from the cinematic entry stage: the whole pl-space div (Mars, dwarf, Moon, Jupiter, the back orbit ring pl-orbits-b, the blinking satellite pl-sat2, the meteor streak) AND the separate sibling pl-orbits-f front orbit ring that flew dots across the front of the globe. Cut B de-cheesed the hero stars: deleted the 4-point diffraction-cross stroke (the +-shaped lens-flare look) from drawStars, keeping the soft radial bloom + center pixel. The 440 background stars, nebula, atmosphere underlay/rim, night WebGL globe, charge ring, and warp-to-Austin entry are all untouched. Stage markup now: pl-stars -> pl-under -> pl-gl -> pl-horizon -> stats -> hint -> flash -> done, zero ornament nodes. Verified no JS referenced the removed elements (CSS-animation-only); the ornament CSS (~10483-10548) is now dead but harmless, left for an optional follow-up sweep (would touch CSS -> brace re-count). Cuts A+B are markup/JS only -> block-0 brace baseline 2760 unaffected. node --check 3/3 inline + sw.js PASS, four stamps lockstep v1.14.35, cache bumped. DEVICE-GATE (John, iPhone): no planets/moon/meteor/satellite/orbit-dots anywhere incl. in front of the globe; stars have no + spikes but field still reads alive; night globe/rim/charge-ring/warp unchanged; FPS holds. Optional next (John's call): §5 realism dials (fewer/smaller hero stars, calmer nebula) + §6 dead-CSS sweep.
